item.title: Royal Caribbean Shares Jump as Outlook Suggests Resilient Demand
item.description: Royal Caribbean Cruises Ltd. shares rose as the company’s outlook for the year suggests it will be able to weather headwinds such as higher fuel costs and disruptions on its Middle Eastern itineraries.

item.title: Chinese Demand Destruction Is Key to Calming Global LNG Prices
item.description: Years of planning helped Beijing to cut imports, freeing up more fuel for other markets.

item.title: Warsh Faces High Hurdle for Rate Cuts as Powell Readies to Pass the Baton
item.description: Jerome Powell says the Fed leadership handover to Kevin Warsh will be “normal.” Some might disagree.

item.title: Polish Rate Cut Chances Are Quickly Evaporating, Dabrowski Says
item.description: Polish interest-rate cut chances significantly diminished after inflation accelerated in April, according to policymaker Ireneusz Dabrowski.

item.title: Ukraine Holds Key Rate as Energy Price Surge Fuels Inflation
item.description: Ukraine held interest rates for a second meeting as policymakers weigh an acceleration in inflation in an economy already battered by more than four years of Russia’s invasion.

item.title: Qualcomm Bids for a Share of the Massive Spending on AI Chips
item.description: The top maker of chips for smartphones wants in on the explosive AI data center market.

item.title: Blue Owl’s Asset Growth Meet Estimates Amid Direct Lending Slump
item.description: Blue Owl Capital Inc.’s fee-related earnings and assets increased as the alternative investment firm leaned on other parts of its business amid souring sentiment toward private credit.

item.title: It’s Boating Season, But Only If You Can Afford Fuel
item.description: In normal times, boaters spend their money at marinas, tackle shops and dockside restaurants, keeping local businesses buzzing. This year, they’re spending it all at the pump.

item.title: Bank of England Holds Rates as Officials Consider Hikes Ahead
item.description: The Bank of England kept interest rates on hold with several policymakers saying they might consider future hikes, just as oil prices soared within reach of the central bank’s most pessimistic scenario for the economy.

item.title: Hormuz Blockade Boosts Inland Container Flows at UAE Port Outside the Strait
item.description: With Hormuz closed, trucks are rerouting containers unable to reach one of the world’s busiest ports

item.title: Hedge Fund Manager ‘Ignoring Trump’ Nets 39% Gain on Energy Bets
item.description: A hedge fund manager focused on navigating the energy transition has generated a 39% return so far this year, after adopting a policy of ignoring US President Donald Trump’s signals on the Iran war.

item.title: SpaceX Investor 137 Raises $700 Million Ahead of Musk-Led IPO
item.description: 137 Ventures, a repeat investor in Elon Musk’s SpaceX, has raised $700 million across two new funds to back startups over longer time horizons.

item.title: ECB Set to Hold Rates as Officials Assess Iran War Impact <!--EndFragment -->
item.description: The European Central Bank headquarters in Frankfurt. Photographer: KIRILL KUDRYAVTSEV/AFP

item.title: Unilever Expects to Raise Detergent Prices on Iran War Costs
item.description: Unilever Plc will likely increase prices for cleaning sprays and laundry detergents in emerging markets due to costs linked to the war in Iran.

item.title: Europe’s Banks Earmark $710 Million to Brace for Iran War Impact
item.description: Europe’s top banks have set aside hundreds of millions of euros to shield against a financial impact from the Iran war, highlighting how the economic risks for the region are on the rise.

item.title: Yen Surges to Two-Month High After Fresh Verbal Intervention
item.description: The yen extended its advance, jumping more than 2% after Japanese officials stepped up verbal support for the currency.

item.title: US Treasuries Rebound Post-Fed as Oil Eases From Four-Year Highs
item.description: US Treasuries gained as Brent crude prices retreated from a four-year high, though simmering inflation fears kept yields near their recent peaks.

item.title: Military Use of NATO Pipeline in Europe Impacts Civilian Supply
item.description: Military operations have impacted sections of a sprawling European fuel pipeline network at a time of supply pressures caused by the Iran war, according to a German service provider for the system.

item.title: Foundering: Bob Lee’s Made-for-TV Murder Trial
item.description: The trial for Bob Lee’s murder became a media circus, including paparazzi photographers, celebrity attorneys, obstinate witnesses, testimony about drug-fueled parties, and secret videos.

item.title: Fed Dissenters Send a Clear Signal to Bond Investors
item.description: Yield on 30-year bond surpasses 5% for first time since July.

item.title: UK Fund AVI Wants Chairman of Japan Drugmaker Rohto to Leave
item.description: Asset Value Investors Ltd. is calling for the chairman of Rohto Pharmaceutical Co. to step down from the Japanese drugmaker’s board of directors, in the latest sign that activist shareholders will stay busy this year.

item.title: Richter CEO Warns Forint Strength Is Hitting Hungary’s Exporters
item.description: Hungary’s post-election market rally is turning into a challenge for Gedeon Richter Nyrt., as a strengthening forint erodes earnings for the export-dependent drugmaker.

item.title: Delta Electronics Plans Rapid Expansion as AI Fuels Growth
item.description: Delta Electronics Inc. plans to accelerate plant expansions to address production bottlenecks after surging demand for artificial intelligence data centers drove first-quarter earnings to a record.

item.title: Botswana Becomes First in Africa to Hike Rates Since Iran War
item.description: Botswana’s central bank became the first in Africa to raise interest rates after the Iran war triggered a global energy-supply shock.

item.title: Adani Flagship Slips Into Quarterly Loss as Costs Jump
item.description: Adani Enterprises Ltd. slipped into a loss in the fourth quarter, as costs jumped amid the company’s push into copper assets and airport expansion.

item.title: Shapoorji Unit Markets High-Yield Debt to JPMorgan, BlackRock
item.description: India’s Shapoorji and Pallonji Group is in talks with global investors including BlackRock and JPMorgan Chase & Co. for a high-yield bond sale, according to people familiar with the matter, even after it was allowed to delay repayment on existing debt.

item.title: Nigeria Taps Ex-Dangote Exec to Head Oil Regulator After Tension
item.description: Nigeria’s president named a former Dangote Cement Plc executive as the next head of the nation’s petroleum-products regulator, the third leadership change in four months at the agency that’s clashed with billionaire investor Aliko Dangote.

item.title: The Messy Reality of AI’s Much Discussed Gender Gap
item.description: Women are using artificial intelligence at lower rates than men. But are they being “left behind”?

item.title: Berkshire’s Ailing Share Price Clouds Abel’s First Annual Meeting as CEO
item.description: Days before his first annual meeting as the chief executive officer of Berkshire Hathaway Inc., Greg Abel is facing a problem that seldom confronted his legendary predecessor: a floundering stock price.

item.title: Two US Senators Seek Cantor Loan Documents From Lutnick and Tether
item.description: Two Democratic senators have asked US Commerce Secretary Howard Lutnick and the cryptocurrency giant Tether for information about a loan between the company and a trust benefitting Lutnick’s children.
